title: foeniculum vulgare subsp. capillaceum
intro: Foeniculum vulgare subsp. capillaceum, also known as wild fennel, is a subspecies of the common fennel plant, characterized by its tall, slender stalks and feathery foliage. The medicinal properties of wild fennel include its carminative, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion, as well as respiratory problems, like coughs and colds. The health benefits of consuming wild fennel include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of menstrual cramps and menopausal hot flashes. Wild fennel is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and seeds in hot water.
